title: Bò Kho

sections.heading: Nguyên liệu · Ingredients
items: 3 lbs beef chuck or shank, cut into large 2-inch cubes
3 stalks lemongrass (sả), bruised and cut into 3-inch pieces
2 tablespoons annatto oil (dầu điều) or 1 tablespoon annatto seeds fried in oil
2 large carrots, cut into thick chunks
1 daikon radish, cut into thick chunks (optional)
Fresh herbs — Thai basil, cilantro, sawtooth herb (ngò gai)
Bean sprouts and lime wedges, for serving
French bread (bánh mì) or rice noodles (phở or hủ tiếu)

sections.heading: Cách làm · Instructions
steps: Cut the beef into large chunks — don't go too small, they'll shrink as they braise. Pat dry with paper towels.
Heat annatto oil in a large heavy pot over medium-high heat.
Fry the sliced shallots, ginger, and lemongrass in the oil until fragrant, about 3–4 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Then add the beef in batches, searing on all sides until browned. Don't skip this step — the sear locks in flavor.
Pour in the chicken broth and coconut water. Add tomato paste, fish sauce, sugar, five-spice powder, cinnamon sticks, and star anise. Stir everything together and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low. Cover and let it simmer gently for 1.5–2 hours. The beef should be tender enough to break apart with chopsticks but still hold its shape.
Add the carrots (and daikon if using) in the last 30 minutes of cooking so they're tender but not mushy.
Taste and adjust — more fish sauce for salt, sugar to balance. The broth should be rich, aromatic, and slightly sweet from the coconut water.
Serve in deep bowls with French bread for dipping, or ladle over rice noodles. Top with fresh herbs, bean sprouts, and a squeeze of lime.

scan_label: Công thức gốc · Original handwritten recipe
